# ScaleFadeAnimation

**Object**

The animation in which a component scales up and fades into view.

## Declaration

```swift
object ScaleFadeAnimation
```

## Discussion

With this animation type the component appears by getting larger and fading from transparent to opaque. Initially, the component is scaled down and at least partially transparent, but, upon entering the user’s view, the component scales up to its actual size in the layout and fades in to be completely opaque as shown in [https://developer.apple.com/apple-news/videos/scale-fade.mp4].

### Example

```json
{
  "components": [
    {
      "role": "figure",
      "URL": "bundle://figure.jpg",
      "animation": {
        "type": "scale_fade",
        "initialAlpha": 0.5,
        "initialScale": 0.75
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Related Documentation

- **Adding Animations**: Use animations to affect how parts of your article come into view the first time they appear.

## Animations

- **About Component Animations**: Learn how to affect the way in which components come into view.
- **ComponentAnimation**: Properties that all types of animations share.
- **AppearAnimation**: An animation type whereby a component appears on the screen.
- **FadeInAnimation**: The animation whereby a component fades into view.
- **MoveInAnimation**: The animation whereby a component moves in from the side of the screen.

## Inherits From

- ComponentAnimation
